title: Crypto Fund Administration Software: What Fund Managers Actually Need
content_html:
Crypto fund administration is the operational work behind a digital-asset fund: tracking positions across wallets and chains, recording who invested what, applying vesting schedules, running distributions, and producing reports investors can trust. Traditional fund admin tools were built for bank accounts and brokerage statements, not for assets spread across dozens of wallets on many chains.

What fund administration covers in crypto

A crypto fund administrator needs a live, accurate answer to four questions at any moment. What does the fund hold, across every wallet and chain? Who are the investors and what are their allocations? What is vested, locked, or pending distribution? And can all of that be shown to an investor or auditor without a week of spreadsheet work?

Doing this by hand means exporting CSVs from block explorers, reconciling them wallet by wallet, and maintaining allocation spreadsheets that break the moment a member joins or a schedule changes.

Where Cryptool fits

Cryptool is an investor management platform that combines portfolio tracking with raise and distribution operations in one account.

  • Portfolio: connect every wallet the fund uses, across all chains and wallet providers, in one non-custodial view. Connections are read only, so the fund never hands over keys.
  • Raise: create pools, record member allocations, define vesting schedules, and run distributions from the same place the positions live.
  • Reporting: because holdings and allocations sit in one system, investor reporting starts from live data instead of reconciled exports.

On-chain modules run on EVM chains and MultiversX, with more coming, while portfolio tracking covers every chain and wallet provider.

Common questions

What is crypto fund administration software?
Software that tracks a digital-asset fund's holdings, investor allocations, vesting, and distributions, and produces investor-ready reporting from that data.

How is it different from a portfolio tracker?
A portfolio tracker answers "what do we hold". Fund administration also answers "who owns it, on what terms, and what do we owe them". Cryptool combines both in one account.

Does Cryptool take custody of fund assets?
No. Wallet connections are read only and non-custodial. Keys stay with the fund.

What does it cost?
Plans are listed on the services and pricing page.
